feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation on SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Core configured for 100 Kbps mode; no other mode under test.
test_steps: Configure prescale/settings for 100 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer.
Measure SCL clock frequency on the bus.
expected_results: SCL operates at 100 Kbps normal-mode rate.
Operation is supported directly by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation on SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: Core configured for 100 Kbps mode; no other mode under test.
test_steps: Configure prescale/settings for 100 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer carrying data on SDA.
Observe SDA activity during the transfer.
expected_results: SDA transfers occur correctly during 100 Kbps normal-mode operation.
Operation is supported directly by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation on SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: Core configured for 400 Kbps mode; no other mode under test.
test_steps: Configure prescale/settings for 400 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer.
Measure SCL clock frequency on the bus.
expected_results: SCL operates at 400 Kbps fast-mode rate.
Operation is supported directly by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must directly support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation on SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: Core configured for 400 Kbps mode; no other mode under test.
test_steps: Configure prescale/settings for 400 Kbps operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer carrying data on SDA.
Observe SDA activity during the transfer.
expected_results: SDA transfers occur correctly during 400 Kbps fast-mode operation.
Operation is supported directly by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_description: Verify presence and operation of the WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Compliance checked only against listed interface signals and classic access behavior provided in the specification.
test_steps: Inspect the interface for wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, and wb_inta_o.
Perform representative WISHBONE read and write accesses using the classic handshake.
Observe that the interface responds through the listed signals.
expected_results: All listed WISHBONE interface signals are exposed.
Read and write accesses operate through the WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a WISHBONE write access takes two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a valid WISHBONE Classic write access.
test_steps: Start a write access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i with wb_we_i high on a rising wb_clk_i edge.
Count clock cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Record total access length in clock cycles.
expected_results: The write access completes in exactly two clock cycles.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a WISHBONE read access takes two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a valid WISHBONE Classic read access.
test_steps: Start a read access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i with wb_we_i low on a rising wb_clk_i edge.
Count clock cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted and read data is returned.
Record total access length in clock cycles.
expected_results: The read access completes in exactly two clock cycles.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active high when ARST_LVL is set high.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to 1'b1.
Drive arst_i high asynchronously.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: Core resets when arst_i is high with ARST_LVL set high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level must be configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active low when ARST_LVL is set low.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to 1'b0.
Drive arst_i low asynchronously.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: Core resets when arst_i is low with ARST_LVL set low.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed.
test_steps: Inspect the external interface of the core.
Check for scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe signals.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are exposed by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals are exposed.
test_steps: Inspect the external interface of the core.
Check for sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe signals.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are exposed by the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: Devices connected to SCL and SDA must use open-drain or open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL connection behavior is compatible with open-drain/open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only behavior observable from provided interface/signals is tested.
test_steps: Connect SCL through an external pull-up/open-drain style bus model.
Command the core to drive and release SCL using its pad controls.
Observe that the line is only pulled low or released.
expected_results: SCL bus behavior is consistent with open-drain/open-collector connection.

feature_list.requirement_text: Devices connected to SCL and SDA must use open-drain or open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA connection behavior is compatible with open-drain/open-collector outputs.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only behavior observable from provided interface/signals is tested.
test_steps: Connect SDA through an external pull-up/open-drain style bus model.
Command the core to drive and release SDA using its pad controls.
Observe that the line is only pulled low or released.
expected_results: SDA bus behavior is consistent with open-drain/open-collector connection.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify scl_pad_oe controls whether the core drives or releases SCL.
feature_list.test_constraints: External pull-up/open-drain style bus model connected.
test_steps: Force a condition where the core should actively drive SCL low and observe scl_pad_oe/scl_pad_o.
Force a condition where the core should release SCL.
Observe resulting SCL bus behavior.
expected_results: When enabled to drive, the core actively asserts the SCL low-drive behavior.
When disabled, the core releases the SCL line.

feature_list.requirement_text: The SCL and SDA output-enable signals must control whether the core drives or releases the external bus lines.
feature_list.test_description: Verify sda_pad_oe controls whether the core drives or releases SDA.
feature_list.test_constraints: External pull-up/open-drain style bus model connected.
test_steps: Force a condition where the core should actively drive SDA low and observe sda_pad_oe/sda_pad_o.
Force a condition where the core should release SDA.
Observe resulting SDA bus behavior.
expected_results: When enabled to drive, the core actively asserts the SDA low-drive behavior.
When disabled, the core releases the SDA line.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERlo must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale low-byte register at address 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x00.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x00.
Read back from address 0x00.
Compare read value with written value.
expected_results: Address 0x00 maps to PRERlo.
PRERlo accepts 8-bit writes and returns the written 8-bit value on read.

feature_list.requirement_text: PRERhi must be an 8-bit read/write Clock Prescale high-byte register at address 0x01.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x01.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x01.
Read back from address 0x01.
Compare read value with written value.
expected_results: Address 0x01 maps to PRERhi.
PRERhi accepts 8-bit writes and returns the written 8-bit value on read.

feature_list.requirement_text: CTR must be an 8-bit read/write Control register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR is an 8-bit read/write register at address 0x02.
feature_list.test_constraints: Reserved bits should be written as zero where applicable.
test_steps: Write a valid 8-bit control value to address 0x02.
Read back from address 0x02.
Compare implemented bits with written value.
expected_results: Address 0x02 maps to CTR.
CTR accepts valid 8-bit writes and implemented bits are readable.

feature_list.requirement_text: TXR must be an 8-bit write-only Transmit register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior is not required for a write-only register.
test_steps: Write an 8-bit test value to address 0x03 with wb_we_i asserted.
Trigger a write transfer that uses TXR contents.
Observe transmitted byte on SDA.
expected_results: Address 0x03 accepts an 8-bit write to TXR.
The written TXR value is used for transmission.

feature_list.requirement_text: RXR must be an 8-bit read-only Receive register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x03.
feature_list.test_constraints: A received byte must be available before reading.
test_steps: Perform an I2C read to receive one byte.
Read address 0x03 with wb_we_i deasserted.
Capture wb_dat_o.
expected_results: Address 0x03 returns RXR on read.
RXR provides the received 8-bit value.

feature_list.requirement_text: CR must be an 8-bit write-only Command register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR is an 8-bit write-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Read behavior is not required for a write-only register.
test_steps: Write a valid command value to address 0x04.
Observe resulting command action on the core or I2C bus.
expected_results: Address 0x04 accepts writes to CR.
Written command bits initiate the commanded action.

feature_list.requirement_text: SR must be an 8-bit read-only Status register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR is an 8-bit read-only register at address 0x04.
feature_list.test_constraints: Create a known status condition before reading.
test_steps: Create a known status condition such as TIP active or IF set.
Read address 0x04.
Capture returned status bits.
expected_results: Address 0x04 returns SR on read.
Returned value reflects current status bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo contributes to SCL configuration through the prescale value.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a known wb_clk_i frequency and program only the low byte contribution in combination with PRERhi.
test_steps: Apply a known wb_clk_i frequency.
Program PRERlo and PRERhi to a target prescale value.
Initiate a transfer and measure resulting SCL frequency.
expected_results: Changing the programmed prescale value changes the SCL clock accordingly.
PRERlo participates in the configured prescale value.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi contributes to SCL configuration through the prescale value.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a known wb_clk_i frequency and vary the high byte contribution.
test_steps: Apply a known wb_clk_i frequency.
Program PRERhi with different values while keeping PRERlo controlled.
Initiate transfers and measure resulting SCL frequency.
expected_results: Changing PRERhi changes the resulting SCL clock according to the programmed prescale value.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register must configure the SCL clock using the WISHBONE input clock and the 5SCL prescale formula.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL frequency follows the programmed prescale relative to wb_clk_i.
test_steps: Apply a known wb_clk_i frequency.
Program a prescale value intended for a target SCL frequency using the 5*SCL minus 1 formula.
Run a transfer and measure SCL frequency.
expected_results: Measured SCL frequency matches the value derived from wb_clk_i and the programmed 5*SCL prescale formula.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register value must only be changed when the EN bit is cleared.
feature_list.test_description: Verify Prescale Register can be changed when EN is cleared.
test_steps: Clear EN in CTR.
Write new values to PRERlo and PRERhi.
Read back PRERlo and PRERhi.
expected_results: Prescale register values update successfully when EN is cleared.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register value must only be changed when the EN bit is cleared.
feature_list.test_description: Verify Prescale Register value is not changed while EN is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: EN must be 1 before write attempt.
test_steps: Set EN in CTR.
Capture current PRERlo and PRERhi values.
Attempt to write new values to PRERlo and PRERhi.
Read back PRERlo and PRERhi.
expected_results: Prescale register value does not change while EN is set.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register reset value must be 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERlo reset value is 0xFF.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read PRERlo at address 0x00.
expected_results: PRERlo resets to 0xFF.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Prescale Register reset value must be 0xFFFF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify PRERhi reset value is 0xFF.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read PRERhi at address 0x01.
expected_results: PRERhi resets to 0xFF.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 7 must be the read/write EN bit used to enable or disable the I2C core.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 7 is the read/write EN bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use valid WISHBONE access to CTR.
test_steps: Write CTR with bit 7 = 1 and other writable bits controlled.
Read back CTR.
Write CTR with bit 7 = 0.
Read back CTR again.
expected_results: CTR bit 7 is writable and readable as EN.
EN=1 enables the core and EN=0 disables the core.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core does not respond to new commands when EN is cleared.
test_steps: Clear EN.
Write a command to CR that would normally initiate bus activity.
Observe internal status and I2C bus signals.
expected_results: No new command is executed while EN is cleared.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must only respond to new commands when EN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core responds to new commands when EN is set.
test_steps: Set EN.
Write a valid command to CR.
Observe status and I2C bus activity.
expected_results: The core accepts and executes the new command when EN is set.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software must only clear EN when no transfer is in progress, after a STOP command, or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify EN can be cleared when no transfer is in progress.
test_steps: Ensure TIP is 0.
Clear EN in CTR.
Read back CTR.
expected_results: EN clears successfully when no transfer is in progress.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software must only clear EN when no transfer is in progress, after a STOP command, or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify EN can be cleared after a STOP command or when STO is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Generate a transfer then issue STOP.
test_steps: Start a transfer.
Issue a STOP command by setting STO.
Clear EN.
Read back CTR.
expected_results: EN clears successfully after STOP command or when STO is set.

feature_list.requirement_text: Control Register bit 6 must be the read/write IEN bit used to enable or disable interrupt generation.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR bit 6 is the read/write IEN bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use valid WISHBONE access to CTR.
test_steps: Write CTR with bit 6 = 1.
Read back CTR.
Write CTR with bit 6 = 0.
Read back CTR again.
expected_results: CTR bit 6 is writable and readable as IEN.
IEN=1 enables interrupt generation and IEN=0 disables interrupt generation.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Control Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CTR reset value is 0x00.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read CTR at address 0x02.
expected_results: CTR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR[7:1] hold the next byte to transmit during data transfer.
test_steps: Write a known 8-bit data value into TXR.
Set WR to initiate transmission.
Observe bits transmitted on SDA.
expected_results: TXR[7:1] are transmitted as the upper seven bits of the byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR bit 0 acts as data LSB during data transfer.
test_steps: Write a known data byte into TXR with a distinctive bit 0 value.
Set WR to initiate data transmission.
Observe the last transmitted data bit on SDA.
expected_results: TXR bit 0 is transmitted as the data LSB during data transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register must hold the byte to transmit, with bit 0 acting as the data LSB during data transfer or the RW bit during slave address transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR bit 0 acts as RW during slave address transfer.
test_steps: Write a 7-bit slave address plus controlled bit 0 into TXR.
Initiate slave address transfer with WR.
Observe the eighth transmitted bit on SDA.
expected_results: TXR bit 0 is used as the RW bit during slave address transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RW=1 indicates reading from the slave during address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use slave address transfer with TXR[0]=1.
test_steps: Load TXR with a 7-bit slave address and bit 0 set to 1.
Initiate address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction.
expected_results: RW value 1 indicates a read-from-slave transaction.

feature_list.requirement_text: During a slave address transfer, RW value 1 must indicate reading from the slave and RW value 0 must indicate writing to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RW=0 indicates writing to the slave during address transfer.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use slave address transfer with TXR[0]=0.
test_steps: Load TXR with a 7-bit slave address and bit 0 set to 0.
Initiate address transfer.
Observe subsequent transfer direction.
expected_results: RW value 0 indicates a write-to-slave transaction.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Transmit Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TXR reset value is 0x00.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Use TXR in a controlled transmission or inspect reset state if directly observable.
expected_results: TXR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains the last byte received via I2C.
feature_list.test_constraints: Perform at least one valid I2C read operation.
test_steps: Read a known byte from an I2C slave.
After transfer completion, read RXR at address 0x03.
Compare RXR value to the received bus byte.
expected_results: RXR contains the last byte received via I2C.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR reset value is 0x00.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read RXR at address 0x03.
expected_results: RXR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 7 must be the write-only STA bit used to generate a START or Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 7 is the write-only STA bit that generates a START condition.
feature_list.test_constraints: Issue STA with a valid transfer command context.
test_steps: Write CR with STA set and appropriate transfer command.
Observe SDA and SCL for START generation.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 7 requests START generation.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 7 must be the write-only STA bit used to generate a START or Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 7 is the write-only STA bit that generates a Repeated START condition.
feature_list.test_constraints: Bus already active without prior STOP.
test_steps: Begin an I2C transaction without issuing STOP.
Write CR with STA set and appropriate transfer command.
Observe SDA and SCL for Repeated START generation.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 7 requests Repeated START generation.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 6 must be the write-only STO bit used to generate a STOP condition.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 6 is the write-only STO bit used to generate STOP.
test_steps: Begin an I2C transaction.
Write CR with STO set.
Observe SDA and SCL for STOP generation.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 6 generates a STOP condition.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 5 must be the write-only RD bit used to initiate a read from the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 5 is the write-only RD bit used to initiate a read.
test_steps: Ensure EN is set and bus is in a valid addressed state for reading.
Write CR with RD set.
Observe that the core performs a read cycle and stores data in RXR.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 5 initiates a read from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 4 must be the write-only WR bit used to initiate a write to the slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 4 is the write-only WR bit used to initiate a write.
test_steps: Ensure EN is set and bus is in a valid addressed state for writing.
Load TXR with a test byte.
Write CR with WR set.
Observe that the byte is transmitted on SDA.
expected_results: Writing CR bit 4 initiates a write to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify ACK=0 causes the receiver to send ACK.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a receive scenario where the core acts as receiver acknowledgment source.
test_steps: Set up a read transfer.
Write CR with ACK bit = 0 in the appropriate command.
Observe the ninth clock acknowledge level on SDA.
expected_results: The core sends ACK when ACK bit is 0.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 3 must be the write-only ACK bit used by a receiver to send ACK when 0 or NACK when 1.
feature_list.test_description: Verify ACK=1 causes the receiver to send NACK.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a receive scenario where the core acts as receiver acknowledgment source.
test_steps: Set up a read transfer.
Write CR with ACK bit = 1 in the appropriate command.
Observe the ninth clock acknowledge level on SDA.
expected_results: The core sends NACK when ACK bit is 1.

feature_list.requirement_text: Command Register bit 0 must be the write-only IACK bit used to clear a pending interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR bit 0 IACK clears a pending interrupt.
test_steps: Create a pending interrupt so IF is set.
Write CR with IACK bit set.
Read SR and observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: Pending interrupt is cleared after IACK write.
IF clears and interrupt request is removed.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STA bit is cleared automatically and reads as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Command register readback observed through register behavior at address 0x04 if supported by design mapping to status/read semantics; read-as-zero aspect taken from requirement text.
test_steps: Write CR with STA set.
Allow command acceptance.
Read back command/status-visible location for command bit read behavior or inspect internal observable behavior if available.
expected_results: STA clears automatically after command acceptance.
STA reads as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STO bit is cleared automatically and reads as zero.
test_steps: Write CR with STO set.
Allow command acceptance.
Read back command/status-visible location for command bit read behavior or inspect internal observable behavior if available.
expected_results: STO clears automatically after command acceptance.
STO reads as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RD bit is cleared automatically and reads as zero.
test_steps: Write CR with RD set.
Allow command acceptance.
Read back command/status-visible location for command bit read behavior or inspect internal observable behavior if available.
expected_results: RD clears automatically after command acceptance.
RD reads as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify WR bit is cleared automatically and reads as zero.
test_steps: Write CR with WR set.
Allow command acceptance.
Read back command/status-visible location for command bit read behavior or inspect internal observable behavior if available.
expected_results: WR clears automatically after command acceptance.
WR reads as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The STA, STO, RD, WR, and IACK command bits must be cleared automatically and must always read as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IACK bit is cleared automatically and reads as zero.
test_steps: Write CR with IACK set.
Allow command acceptance.
Read back command/status-visible location for command bit read behavior or inspect internal observable behavior if available.
expected_results: IACK clears automatically after command acceptance.
IACK reads as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Command Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify CR reset value is 0x00.
feature_list.test_constraints: CR is write-only; reset verification may require indirect observation.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Observe that no command is pending and no command action occurs until software writes CR.
expected_results: CR reset state is consistent with 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK=0 indicates acknowledge received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a slave that acknowledges address/data.
test_steps: Perform a transfer to a responding slave.
Read SR after the acknowledge phase.
Inspect RxACK bit.
expected_results: RxACK is 0 when acknowledge is received.

feature_list.requirement_text: RxACK must indicate whether acknowledge was received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RxACK=1 indicates no acknowledge received from the addressed slave.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a non-responding slave address or force NACK.
test_steps: Perform a transfer that is not acknowledged.
Read SR after the acknowledge phase.
Inspect RxACK bit.
expected_results: RxACK is 1 when no acknowledge is received.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY sets after START detection.
test_steps: Generate a START condition on the bus.
Read SR after START detection.
Inspect BUSY bit.
expected_results: BUSY is set to 1 after START detection.

feature_list.requirement_text: BUSY must indicate whether the I2C bus is busy, setting after START detection and clearing after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_description: Verify BUSY clears after STOP detection.
feature_list.test_constraints: Valid STOP condition required after bus active state.
test_steps: Ensure bus is busy.
Generate a STOP condition on the bus.
Read SR after STOP detection.
Inspect BUSY bit.
expected_results: BUSY is cleared to 0 after STOP detection.

feature_list.requirement_text: AL must indicate arbitration lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL sets when arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_constraints: Multi-master or forced arbitration-loss scenario required.
test_steps: Create an arbitration-loss condition.
Read SR.
Inspect AL bit.
expected_results: AL is set when the core loses arbitration.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when a STOP signal is detected but was not requested.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL sets when an unrequested STOP is detected.
feature_list.test_constraints: Generate STOP on bus without core requesting STO.
test_steps: Begin a transfer without requesting STOP.
Externally force or detect a STOP condition on the bus.
Read SR.
Inspect AL bit.
expected_results: AL is set when an unrequested STOP is detected.

feature_list.requirement_text: Arbitration must be considered lost when the master drives SDA high but SDA is low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify AL sets when master drives SDA high but observed SDA is low.
test_steps: Create a condition where the core releases/drives SDA high semantics via open-drain release.
Force sda_pad_i low externally during that bit time.
Read SR.
Inspect AL bit.
expected_results: AL is set when intended SDA high is observed low on the bus.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP=1 while a transfer is in progress.
test_steps: Start an I2C read or write transaction.
Read SR during byte transfer.
Inspect TIP bit.
expected_results: TIP is 1 while data transfer is in progress.

feature_list.requirement_text: TIP must indicate whether a transfer is in progress.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP=0 when transfer is complete.
test_steps: Complete an I2C read or write transaction.
Read SR after transfer completion.
Inspect TIP bit.
expected_results: TIP is 0 when transfer is complete.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must indicate whether an interrupt is pending.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF sets when an interrupt is pending.
test_steps: Cause a byte transfer completion or arbitration loss.
Read SR.
Inspect IF bit.
expected_results: IF is set when an interrupt is pending.

feature_list.requirement_text: A pending interrupt must cause a processor interrupt request when IEN is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify pending interrupt asserts wb_inta_o when IEN is set.
test_steps: Set IEN.
Cause a pending interrupt condition so IF sets.
Observe wb_inta_o.
expected_results: wb_inta_o is asserted when IF is set and IEN is enabled.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF is set when one byte transfer has completed.
test_steps: Perform a single byte transfer.
Read SR after completion.
Inspect IF bit.
expected_results: IF is set when one byte transfer has completed.

feature_list.requirement_text: IF must be set when one byte transfer has completed or arbitration is lost.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF is set when arbitration is lost.
test_steps: Cause arbitration loss.
Read SR.
Inspect IF bit.
expected_results: IF is set when arbitration is lost.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Status Register reset value must be 0x00.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SR reset value is 0x00.
test_steps: Apply reset.
Read SR at address 0x04.
expected_results: SR resets to 0x00.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved status bits SR[4:2] read as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Any operating state; reserved bits only.
test_steps: Read SR in one or more operating conditions.
Inspect bits [4:2].
expected_results: SR[4:2] read as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved command bits CR[2:1] read as zero.
feature_list.test_constraints: Readback may be indirect because CR is write-only in map.
test_steps: Write zeros to CR[2:1] as required by software convention.
Observe no defined function is associated with these bits; if read visibility exists, inspect as zero.
expected_results: CR reserved bits are treated as zero for read/compatibility purposes and are written as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: Reserved bits must read as zero, and writable reserved bits should be written as zero.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reserved control bits CTR[5:0] are written as zero where reserved.
feature_list.test_constraints: Bit 6 and bit 7 are defined; only reserved subset of CTR[5:0] applies from text.
test_steps: Write CTR with reserved bits in [5:0] cleared to zero.
Read back CTR.
Inspect reserved positions.
expected_results: Reserved control bits are maintained as zero when written as zero.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data must be transferred between a Master and Slave synchronously to SCL on SDA, byte by byte.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA data transfer is synchronous to SCL.
test_steps: Perform an I2C transaction.
Observe SDA transitions and sampling relative to SCL.
expected_results: Data transfer on SDA occurs synchronously with SCL.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data must be transferred between a Master and Slave synchronously to SCL on SDA, byte by byte.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data is transferred byte by byte.
feature_list.test_constraints: Valid transfer of multiple bytes required.
test_steps: Perform a multi-byte I2C transfer.
Count groups of transmitted bits between acknowledge cycles.
expected_results: Transfer occurs in 8-bit byte units separated by acknowledge cycles.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each I2C data byte is 8 bits long.
test_steps: Transmit or receive a byte over I2C.
Count data bit clocks before the acknowledge clock.
expected_results: Each data byte consists of 8 bits.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each I2C data byte must be 8 bits long and transmitted most significant bit first.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each I2C data byte is transmitted MSB first.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a test byte with distinguishable bit order.
test_steps: Load or expect a known byte pattern.
Perform the transfer.
Observe bit order on SDA over the first 8 SCL pulses.
expected_results: The most significant bit is transmitted first.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each transferred byte must be followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify each transferred byte is followed by an acknowledge bit.
test_steps: Perform a byte transfer.
Observe SDA during the acknowledge phase after 8 data bits.
expected_results: An acknowledge bit follows each transferred byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: Each transferred byte must be followed by an acknowledge bit on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the acknowledge bit occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.
test_steps: Perform a byte transfer.
Count SCL pulses for the byte and following acknowledge phase.
Check timing of acknowledge relative to pulse count.
expected_results: The acknowledge bit occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data bits are sampled during SCL high.
test_steps: Perform a data transfer.
Observe data validity relative to SCL high period.
expected_results: Data bits are sampled during the high period of SCL.

feature_list.requirement_text: Data bits must be sampled during SCL high, and SDA must remain stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA remains stable while SCL is high during data transfer.
test_steps: Perform a data transfer.
Monitor SDA only during SCL high periods.
expected_results: SDA remains stable while SCL is high during data transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a standard I2C communication starts with START.
test_steps: Initiate a standard I2C transaction.
Observe first protocol event on the bus.
expected_results: The transaction begins with START.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer follows START in a standard I2C communication.
test_steps: Initiate a standard I2C transaction.
Observe the event immediately after START.
expected_results: Slave address transfer follows START.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer follows slave address transfer in a standard I2C communication.
test_steps: Initiate a standard I2C transaction.
Observe bus activity after slave address phase.
expected_results: Data transfer follows slave address transfer.

feature_list.requirement_text: A standard I2C communication must follow the sequence START, slave address transfer, data transfer, and STOP.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STOP terminates a standard I2C communication.
test_steps: Initiate and complete a standard I2C transaction.
Observe the final protocol event on the bus.
expected_results: The transaction ends with STOP.

feature_list.requirement_text: A START signal must be a high-to-low transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify START is a high-to-low SDA transition while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a START condition on the bus.
test_steps: Generate a START condition.
Monitor SDA and SCL during the event.
expected_results: START is observed as SDA transitioning high-to-low while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: A Repeated START must be a START signal generated without first generating a STOP signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify Repeated START is generated without a preceding STOP.
test_steps: Start a transaction.
Without issuing STOP, request another START.
Observe bus sequence.
expected_results: A START condition is generated without a prior STOP, constituting a Repeated START.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core generates START when STA and RD are set.
test_steps: Write CR with STA=1 and RD=1.
Observe SDA and SCL.
expected_results: The core generates a START signal when STA and RD are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must generate a START signal when STA is set and either RD or WR is set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the core generates START when STA and WR are set.
test_steps: Write CR with STA=1 and WR=1.
Observe SDA and SCL.
expected_results: The core generates a START signal when STA and WR are set.

feature_list.requirement_text: A STOP signal must be a low-to-high transition of SDA while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_description: Verify STOP is a low-to-high SDA transition while SCL is high.
feature_list.test_constraints: Observe a STOP condition on the bus.
test_steps: Generate a STOP condition.
Monitor SDA and SCL during the event.
expected_results: STOP is observed as SDA transitioning low-to-high while SCL is high.

feature_list.requirement_text: The first byte transferred by the master after a START signal must be the slave address with a seven-bit address followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the first byte after START is the slave address.
test_steps: Initiate a transaction with START.
Observe the first transferred byte after START.
expected_results: The first byte after START is the slave address byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: The first byte transferred by the master after a START signal must be the slave address with a seven-bit address followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the slave address byte contains seven address bits followed by an RW bit.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use known address and RW setting.
test_steps: Initiate address transfer with known 7-bit address and RW value.
Observe the 8 transmitted bits on SDA.
expected_results: Bits [7:1] are the seven-bit address and bit [0] is the RW bit.

feature_list.requirement_text: The addressed slave must acknowledge by pulling SDA low at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify the addressed slave pulls SDA low to acknowledge.
test_steps: Transmit a slave address to a matching slave.
Observe SDA during the acknowledge phase.
expected_results: The addressed slave pulls SDA low to acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: The addressed slave must acknowledge by pulling SDA low at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave acknowledge occurs at the ninth SCL clock cycle.
test_steps: Transmit a slave address to a matching slave.
Count SCL clocks from the start of the address byte through the acknowledge phase.
expected_results: The slave acknowledge occurs on the ninth SCL clock cycle.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must support 10-bit slave addresses by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_description: Verify 10-bit slave addressing generates the first address transfer.
test_steps: Initiate a 10-bit addressed transaction.
Observe the first address transfer byte sequence.
expected_results: The core generates the first address transfer for 10-bit addressing.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must support 10-bit slave addresses by generating two address transfers.
feature_list.test_description: Verify 10-bit slave addressing generates the second address transfer.
test_steps: Continue the 10-bit addressed transaction after the first address transfer.
Observe the second address transfer byte sequence.
expected_results: The core generates the second address transfer for 10-bit addressing.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must treat a slave address transfer as a write action using the Transmit Register and WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer uses TXR contents.
test_steps: Write a known slave address byte into TXR.
Set WR to initiate transfer.
Observe the address byte on SDA.
expected_results: The core transfers the slave address stored in TXR.

feature_list.requirement_text: The core must treat a slave address transfer as a write action using the Transmit Register and WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify slave address transfer is initiated using WR bit.
test_steps: Load TXR with a slave address byte.
Set WR.
Observe that the core begins address transmission.
expected_results: The core treats slave address transfer as a write action initiated by WR.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify data transfer proceeds byte by byte after successful slave addressing.
test_steps: Address a slave successfully.
Perform subsequent data transfer.
Observe grouping of transferred data.
expected_results: Data transfer proceeds byte by byte after successful slave addressing.

feature_list.requirement_text: After successful slave addressing, data transfer must proceed byte by byte in the direction specified by the RW bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer direction follows RW bit after successful slave addressing.
feature_list.test_constraints: Run one RW=0 case and one RW=1 case.
test_steps: Perform a transaction with RW=0 after successful addressing.
Observe write direction.
Perform a transaction with RW=1 after successful addressing.
Observe read direction.
expected_results: RW=0 results in write direction and RW=1 results in read direction.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify master can generate STOP after slave No Acknowledge.
feature_list.test_constraints: Force or use a NACKing slave.
test_steps: Perform a transfer that results in RxACK indicating no acknowledge.
Issue STO.
Observe bus behavior.
expected_results: The master can generate STOP after No Acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave signals No Acknowledge, the master may generate a STOP or Repeated START signal.
feature_list.test_description: Verify master can generate Repeated START after slave No Acknowledge.
feature_list.test_constraints: Force or use a NACKing slave.
test_steps: Perform a transfer that results in RxACK indicating no acknowledge.
Issue STA with a new transfer command.
Observe bus behavior.
expected_results: The master can generate Repeated START after No Acknowledge.

feature_list.requirement_text: To write data to a slave, software must store the data in the Transmit Register and set the WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing data to a slave uses TXR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software-sequence requirement validated by functional behavior.
test_steps: Write a known data byte into TXR.
Issue WR.
Observe data on SDA.
expected_results: Data written to TXR is the data transmitted to the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: To write data to a slave, software must store the data in the Transmit Register and set the WR bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify writing data to a slave is initiated by setting WR.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software-sequence requirement validated by functional behavior.
test_steps: Load TXR with a known data byte.
Set WR.
Observe start of byte transmission.
expected_results: Setting WR initiates transmission of the TXR data byte.

feature_list.requirement_text: To read data from a slave, software must set the RD bit.
feature_list.test_description: Verify reading data from a slave is initiated by setting RD.
feature_list.test_constraints: Software-sequence requirement validated by functional behavior.
test_steps: Place the bus in a valid addressed read state.
Set RD.
Observe incoming data and RXR update.
expected_results: Setting RD initiates a read from the slave.

feature_list.requirement_text: When a transfer is done, the core must reset TIP and set IF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify TIP resets when transfer is done.
test_steps: Start and complete a transfer.
Read SR after completion.
Inspect TIP.
expected_results: TIP is reset when the transfer is done.

feature_list.requirement_text: When a transfer is done, the core must reset TIP and set IF.
feature_list.test_description: Verify IF sets when transfer is done.
test_steps: Start and complete a transfer.
Read SR after completion.
Inspect IF.
expected_results: IF is set when the transfer is done.

feature_list.requirement_text: When interrupt generation is enabled and a transfer is done, the core must generate an interrupt.
feature_list.test_description: Verify transfer completion generates wb_inta_o when interrupt generation is enabled.
test_steps: Set IEN.
Perform and complete a transfer.
Observe wb_inta_o after completion.
expected_results: The core generates an interrupt on wb_inta_o when the transfer completes and interrupts are enabled.

feature_list.requirement_text: The Receive Register must contain valid data after IF has been set.
feature_list.test_description: Verify RXR contains valid data after IF is set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Use a read transfer that returns known data.
test_steps: Perform a read transfer until IF sets.
Read RXR.
Compare RXR to expected received data.
expected_results: RXR contains valid received data after IF is set.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software may issue a new read or write command when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a new read command may be issued when TIP is reset.
test_steps: Complete any current transfer and confirm TIP=0.
Write a new command with RD set.
Observe command acceptance.
expected_results: A new read command is accepted when TIP is reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: Software may issue a new read or write command when TIP is reset.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a new write command may be issued when TIP is reset.
test_steps: Complete any current transfer and confirm TIP=0.
Write a new command with WR set.
Observe command acceptance.
expected_results: A new write command is accepted when TIP is reset.

feature_list.requirement_text: When two or more masters try to control the bus simultaneously, clock synchronization must determine the bus clock.
feature_list.test_description: Verify simultaneous masters use clock synchronization to determine SCL bus clock.
test_steps: Operate two masters simultaneously on the bus with different internal clock timing.
Observe resulting SCL bus waveform.
expected_results: The bus clock is determined by clock synchronization under simultaneous multi-master control.

feature_list.requirement_text: During clock synchronization, SCL must be held low by the device with the longest low period and held high by the device with the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL low period is held by the device with the longest low period during synchronization.
feature_list.test_constraints: Multi-master environment with different low periods required.
test_steps: Set up two masters with different SCL low periods.
Run simultaneous bus control.
Measure resulting SCL low period.
expected_results: SCL remains low for the longest master low period.

feature_list.requirement_text: During clock synchronization, SCL must be held low by the device with the longest low period and held high by the device with the shortest high period.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL high period is limited by the device with the shortest high period during synchronization.
feature_list.test_constraints: Multi-master environment with different high periods required.
test_steps: Set up two masters with different SCL high periods.
Run simultaneous bus control.
Measure resulting SCL high period.
expected_results: SCL high duration corresponds to the shortest master high period.

feature_list.requirement_text: Slave devices may slow the transfer bit rate by holding SCL low after the master has driven SCL low.
feature_list.test_description: Verify a slave can hold SCL low after the master drives SCL low.
test_steps: Start a transfer to a slave capable of clock stretching.
After the master pulls SCL low, have the slave hold SCL low longer.
Observe SCL on the bus.
expected_results: The slave holds SCL low after the master has driven SCL low.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave SCL low period is greater than the master SCL low period, the resulting SCL bus low period must be stretched and wait states inserted.
feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL low period is stretched when slave low period exceeds master low period.
test_steps: Configure a scenario where the slave holds SCL low longer than the master's programmed low period.
Run a transfer and measure bus SCL low duration.
expected_results: The resulting SCL bus low period is stretched beyond the master's nominal low period.

feature_list.requirement_text: If the slave SCL low period is greater than the master SCL low period, the resulting SCL bus low period must be stretched and wait states inserted.
feature_list.test_description: Verify wait states are inserted when slave stretches SCL low.
test_steps: Configure a scenario where the slave stretches SCL low.
Run a transfer and observe transfer timing progression.
expected_results: Wait states are inserted while the bus remains in the stretched low period.
